title: EVALUATING MACHINE AND PROCESS PERFORMANCE IN DISTRIBUTED SYSTEM
summary:
Performance of a collection of machines, arranged in a linear sequence of machines that form a linear communication orbit (LCO), is monitored. Multiple machines in the LCO receive, via the LCO, a set of rules (or various subsets of the same set of rules), each rule specifying one or a combination of conditions (e.g., a performance metric and corresponding criterion) for satisfying the rule, evaluate those rules with respect to locally occurring events and local processes, and store results of those evaluations in a local database. In response to a query sent to the machines via the LCO, each of the machines returns a report, including information identifying processes whose performance during the specified time period satisfies at least one rule in the set of one or more rules. Those reports are aggregated and used to generate a merged report reflecting performance information with respect to a set of machines.

ABSTRACT
Performance of a collection of machines, arranged in a linear sequence of machines that form a linear communication orbit (LCO), is monitored. Multiple machines in the LCO receive, via the LCO, a set of rules (or various subsets of the same set of rules), each rule specifying one or a combination of conditions (e.g., a performance metric and corresponding criterion) for satisfying the rule, evaluate those rules with respect to locally occurring events and local processes, and store results of those evaluations in a local database. In response to a query sent to the machines via the LCO, each of the machines returns a report, including information identifying processes whose performance during the specified time period satisfies at least one rule in the set of one or more rules. Those reports are aggregated and used to generate a merged report reflecting performance information with respect to a set of machines.
1
details:
1. A method, comprising:

 at a server system in communication with a plurality of managed machines, the plurality of managed machines self-organized into a linear communication orbit by coordinating with neighbor machines:

 causing each respective machine of the plurality of managed machines to automatically distribute software updates associated with the respective machine, each respective machine to responsively store the software updates and associated metadata in accordance with one or more rules, the software updates associated with one or more software programs executed by the respective machine;

 transmitting a query to a first machine of the plurality of managed machines, the plurality of managed machines, responsive to the query, to collectively generate a merged report comprising aggregated software update metrics and associated metadata indicated by the query; and

 receiving the merged report from a last machine of the plurality of managed machines in the linear communication orbit.

 2

 2. The method of claim 1
, wherein respective portions of the software update metrics and associated metadata are associated with a particular period of time indicated by the query.

 3

 3. The method of claim 1
, wherein each respective machine following the first machine in the linear communication orbit is to receive a message corresponding to the query from a neighbor machine along the linear communication orbit, the message indicating respective portions of the software update metrics and associated metadata to be added to the message by the respective machine.

 4

 4. The method of claim 1
, wherein the software update metrics and associated metadata includes machine-specific software update metrics and associated metadata corresponding to the respective machine and aggregated software update metrics and associated metadata aggregated from all or a subset of the plurality of managed machines.

 5

 5. The method of claim 1
, wherein the plurality of managed machines are to collectively generate the merged report by:

 generating a first report by the first machine, the first report comprising a respective portion of the software update metrics and associated metadata indicated by the query;

 transmitting by the first machine and each successive machine in the linear communication orbit, a message including the first report to a respective neighbor machine in the linear communication orbit;

 adding to the first report by each machine following the first machine in the linear communication orbit, a respective portion of the software update metrics and associated metadata indicated by the query; and

 generating the merged report by the last machine of the plurality of managed machines by adding its respective portion of the software update metrics and associated metadata to the first report.

 6

 6. The method of claim 2
, wherein the associated metadata comprises event metadata related to monitored events on the respective machine.

 7

 7. The method of claim 6
, wherein the event metadata includes an event type and a date and time associated with each respective event, the date and time within the particular period of time indicated by the query.

 8

 8. The method of claim 7
, wherein the event metadata further includes data associated with processes executed by the respective machine.

 9

 9. The method of claim 8
, wherein the event metadata further includes a machine identifier (ID) to indicate a respective machine, and parameters indicating that one or more of the software updates failed a test specified by a corresponding rule of the one or more rules.

 10

 10. The method of claim 9
, wherein the parameters are to further indicate one or more of: application malfunctions, communication network impairments, and/or events that violate predefined rules.

 11

 11. A system in communication with a plurality of managed machines, the plurality of managed machines self-organized into a linear communication orbit by coordinating with neighbor machines, the system comprising:

 a set of one or more processors; and

 a non-transitory computer readable storage medium that provides instructions that, when executed by one or any combination of the set one or more processors, causes the system to perform operations comprising: 

 causing each respective machine of the plurality of managed machines to automatically distribute software updates associated with the respective machine, each respective machine to responsively store the software updates and associated metadata in accordance with one or more rules, the software updates associated with one or more software programs executed by the respective machine;

 transmitting a query to a first machine of the plurality of managed machines, the plurality of managed machines, responsive to the query, to collectively generate a merged report comprising aggregated software update metrics and associated metadata indicated by the query; and

 receiving the merged report from a last machine of the plurality of managed machines in the linear communication orbit.

 12

 12. The system of claim 11
, wherein respective portions of the software update metrics and associated metadata are associated with a particular period of time indicated by the query.

 13

 13. The system of claim 11
, wherein each respective machine following the first machine in the linear communication orbit is to receive a message corresponding to the query from a neighbor machine along the linear communication orbit, the message indicating respective portions of the software update metrics and associated metadata to be added to the message by the respective machine.

 14

 14. The system of claim 11
, wherein the software update metrics and associated metadata includes machine-specific software update metrics and associated metadata corresponding to the respective machine and aggregated software update metrics and associated metadata aggregated from all or a subset of the plurality of managed machines.

 15

 15. The system of claim 11
, wherein the plurality of managed machines are to collectively generate the merged report by:

 generating a first report by the first machine, the first report comprising a respective portion of the software update metrics and associated metadata indicated by the query;

 transmitting by the first machine and each successive machine in the linear communication orbit, a message including the first report to a respective neighbor machine in the linear communication orbit;

 adding to the first report by each machine following the first machine in the linear communication orbit, a respective portion of the software update metrics and associated metadata indicated by the query; and

 generating the merged report by the last machine of the plurality of managed machines by adding its respective portion of the software update metrics and associated metadata to the first report.

 16

 16. The system of claim 12
, wherein the associated metadata comprises event metadata related to monitored events on the respective machine.

 17

 17. The system of claim 16
, wherein the event metadata includes an event type and a date and time associated with each respective event, the date and time within the particular period of time indicated by the query.

 18

 18. The system of claim 17
, wherein the event metadata further includes data associated with processes executed by the respective machine.

 19

 19. The system of claim 18
, wherein the event metadata further includes a machine identifier (ID) to indicate a respective machine, and parameters indicating that one or more of the software updates failed a test specified by a corresponding rule of the one or more rules.

 20

 20. The system of claim 19
, wherein the parameters are to further indicate one or more of: application malfunctions, communication network impairments, and/or events that violate predefined rules.
